markers.evidence_type: p75 neurotrophin receptor, defining marker of non-myelinating/Remak Schwann cells
glial fibrillary acidic protein, non-myelinating Schwann cell marker
glutamine synthetase, non-myelinating Schwann cell marker
apolipoprotein D, non-myelinating Schwann cell marker
fatty acid binding protein 7 (brain type), non-myelinating Schwann cell marker
SPARC-related modular calcium-binding protein 2, non-myelinating Schwann cell marker

other_findings.finding: Non-myelinating Schwann cells (Schwann_2) are defined by NGFR, GFAP, GLUL, APOD, FABP7, and SMOC2 expression, with absent or low expression of myelin genes MPZ and MBP
Non-myelinating (Remak) Schwann cells form Remak bundles that ensheath multiple unmyelinated C-fibres including nociceptors and autonomic fibres
NGFR (p75NTR) mediates neurotrophin signalling in non-myelinating Schwann cells and is important for their maintenance and response to nerve injury

evidence_quotes.quote: Non-myelinating Schwann cells express GLUL, APOD, NGFR, SMOC2, FABP7 and are distinguished from myelinating Schwann cells by low/absent MPZ and MBP expression
evidence_quotes.source_file: Zhang et al. (2025) Single-cell atlas of peripheral nervous system cells in human skin

evidence_quotes.quote: NGFR (p75NTR) is a defining marker of Remak (non-myelinating) Schwann cells and distinguishes them from myelinating Schwann cells in peripheral nerves and skin
evidence_quotes.source_file: Ioghen et al. (2020) Molecular aspects of peripheral myelination
evidence_quotes.context: NGFR as non-myelinating Schwann cell marker
